name: LEGO
fact: The name comes from Danish 'leg godt' meaning 'play well', reflecting its focus on creative building.

name: IKEA
fact: Its name combines the founder’s initials with his farm and village, grounding the global brand in rural Sweden.

name: Nokia
fact: It began as a paper mill in the 1800s before evolving into one of the world’s most influential mobile phone companies.

name: Pho
fact: This noodle soup emerged in northern Vietnam in the late 1800s, blending local traditions with French colonial influence.

name: Boomerang
fact: Used by Aboriginal Australians for thousands of years, not all boomerangs were designed to return.

name: Flamenco
fact: Originating in Andalusia, flamenco blends Romani, Moorish, and Spanish influences into music, dance, and song.

name: World Wide Web
fact: Invented in 1989 by Tim Berners-Lee to help scientists share information across computers.

name: Matryoshka Dolls
fact: First made in 1890, these nesting dolls symbolize layers of family and generations.

name: Playing Cards
fact: Early forms appeared in China over 1,000 years ago, possibly evolving from paper money and games of chance.

name: WiFi
fact: Key wireless communication breakthroughs by Dutch engineers helped make modern WiFi possible.

name: Lock and Key
fact: Ancient Egyptians created early wooden pin-tumbler locks, the basis for many modern designs.

name: Newspaper
fact: The first printed newspapers appeared in 17th-century Germany as printing technology spread across Europe.

name: Blue Jeans
fact: Invented in 1873 as durable workwear for miners, they later became a global fashion staple.

name: Shampoo
fact: The word comes from Hindi 'chāmpo' meaning 'to massage', referring to traditional hair cleansing practices.

name: Havaianas
fact: Inspired by Japanese sandals, they became a global symbol of casual summer footwear.

name: Injera
fact: Made from teff, this sour fermented flatbread also acts as a utensil for scooping food.

name: Adidas
fact: Founded in 1949, its three-stripe design became one of the most recognizable symbols in sportswear.

name: Cinema
fact: The Lumière brothers’ Cinématographe could record, project, and display moving images, launching modern film.

name: Nutella
fact: Created after WWII using hazelnuts to stretch limited cocoa supplies, it became a global spread.

name: Bluetooth
fact: Named after a Viking king, it was developed to connect devices wirelessly over short distances.

name: Saxophone
fact: Invented in the 1840s, it was designed to bridge brass and woodwind sounds.

name: Rubik’s Cube
fact: Originally a teaching tool, it became one of the best-selling puzzles in history.

name: Ballpoint Pen
fact: Designed for smoother writing, it replaced fountain pens by using quick-drying ink.

name: Alarm Clock
fact: Early versions used water clocks to trigger sounds at specific times.

name: Buttons
fact: Used in the Indus Valley over 4,000 years ago, initially for decoration rather than fastening.

name: Fez Hat
fact: Named after the city of Fez, it became widely worn across the Ottoman world.

name: Nazar Amulet
fact: Common across the region, it is believed to protect against harmful envy known as the 'evil eye'.

name: Coffee
fact: Legend says a goat herder noticed energized goats after eating coffee berries, leading to its discovery.

name: Sriracha
fact: Named after the coastal city of Si Racha, it later became globally popular in different variations.

name: Quinoa
fact: Cultivated in the Andes for thousands of years, it was a staple crop of the Inca civilization.

name: Kites
fact: Originally used for military signaling and measuring distances before becoming recreational.

name: Ukulele (Not the Guitar)
fact: Developed in Hawaii from Portuguese instruments brought by immigrants in the 19th century.

name: Cigarette
fact: Tobacco use spread globally after Europeans encountered Indigenous smoking traditions in the Americas.

name: Safari
fact: From the Swahili word for 'journey', it later came to mean wildlife expeditions in East Africa.

name: Tempura
fact: Portuguese missionaries introduced frying techniques to Japan, where tempura evolved into its modern form.

name: Contraceptive Pill
fact: Key hormones were first synthesized from compounds found in Mexican yams.

name: Wine
fact: Archaeological evidence shows winemaking here dates back over 8,000 years.

name: Necktie
fact: Inspired by scarves worn by Croatian soldiers, later adopted in French fashion.

name: Red Bull
fact: Based on the Thai drink Krating Daeng, originally popular among laborers and drivers.

name: Kerosene Lamp
fact: The first practical kerosene lamp made safe indoor lighting widely accessible in the 19th century.

name: Velcro
fact: Invented after observing burrs sticking to clothing, mimicking their hook-and-loop structure.

name: MP3 Player
fact: Early portable MP3 players helped transform how people carried and listened to music.

name: QR Code
fact: Created in 1994 to track car parts, it can store far more data than traditional barcodes.

name: Fountain Pen
fact: An early modern version was patented by Petrache Poenaru to provide a continuous ink flow.
